case_title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA VS. JASON YIP ET AL

docket_entries.description: DEMAND FOR JURY (TRANSACTION ID # 64780982) FILED BY DEFENDANT YIP, JASON

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT JASON YIP'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING, OR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS: NO OPPOSITION FILED; THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT NOT ONLY WAS THIS MOTION NOT OPPOSED, PLAINTIFF VIOLATED A PRIOR COURT ORDER COMPELLING DISCOVERY RESPONSES. MOREOVER, THE MOTION THAT LED TO THAT ORDER, WAS ALSO NOT OPPOSED. FROM ALL OF THE EVIDENCE, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ABANDONED THIS CASE, AND THEREFORE, THE CASE IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE PURSUANT TO CCP 2023.030(D)(3). THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING/MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO DAVIDIDAHO14@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-05-2021 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Or Evidentiary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Robert M. Maltz (robert.maltz@farmersinsurance.com) of Stratman, Schwartz & Williams-Abrego for Defendant/Moving Party Jason Yip.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JASON YIP'S Motion For Terminating, Or Evidentiary Sanctions:

No opposition filed; the motion for terminating sanctions is granted. The Court finds that not only was this motion not opposed, Plaintiff violated a prior court order compelling discovery responses. Moreover, the motion that led to that order, was also not opposed. From all of the evidence, it appears that Plaintiff has abandoned this case, and therefore, the case is dismissed with prejudice pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(3). The request for monetary sanctions is denied.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JASON YIP's Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Jason Yip's unopposed Motion to Compel Answers to Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production is GRANTED. Verified responses to the discovery at issue and responsive documents shall be served within two weeks of notice of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $510 are awarded in favor of Defendant Yip and against Plaintiff The Regents of the University of California. Sanctions are due within two weeks of notice of this order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported (302/JPT)
